prompt: Add comment support to the KVR package at ./kvr/ — comments are lines starting with `#` and run to end-of-line (per SPEC.md's Lexical Elements and Semantics sections). Comments above a Record or Block must round-trip cleanly: the parser captures them as leading trivia attached to the next AST node, and the printer emits them before the node's own output. Add a TokenComment token type, attach a LeadingComments []string field to Record and Block, and update parser+printer accordingly. Tests should cover: a comment above a record, a blank-line then a comment then a record, two comments above the same record, and a round-trip for each case. Don't read the whole SPEC.md.

assertions.text: tokenizer.go defines a TokenComment constant in the TokenType const block
tokenizer.go has an action (function or closure) that consumes # to end-of-line and yields a TokenComment
parser.go's Record struct has a LeadingComments []string field (or an equivalent trivia attachment that survives round-trip)

parser.go captures TokenComment tokens before a record and stores them on the next AST node (look for code that reads TokenComment and appends to a slice that flushes when the next non-comment token arrives)
printer.go emits the LeadingComments before the record's own output (look for a write call inside printRecord that iterates comments before printing the record body)
printer_test.go (or a parser+printer interaction test) has a round-trip subtest with one comment above a record
printer_test.go has a round-trip subtest with at least two comments above the same record
tokenizer_test.go has a test for a comment token that asserts an exact Pos value for the comment
all new tests call t.Parallel() at both function and subtest level
no scratch copy of SPEC.md content was written to the package

SKILL.md specifies, for both _context_tokens.md and _context_ast.md, a strict signature-only format (no rationale, no examples), a hard 400-line cap, and a chunk-and-relaunch protocol when the cap would be exceeded (issue #46 acceptance)

SKILL.md specifies the issue #47 phase-chunking protocol: an up-front scope gate in `Before you start` (numeric threshold, partitioning into sub-units along section boundaries, plan announced to the user before any subagent launches) and a `## Phase chunking` section saying sub-calls run serially, append to the running _context_tokens.md / _context_ast.md, and do not full-read the growing tokenizer.go / parser.go / printer.go source file (issue #47 acceptance)
partition_plan.md exists in the package and reports `no partitioning needed` for every phase (kvr is below the 600-line gate, so no phase should partition)
